issues.current_text: a new array containing all elements of this `AnyAccumulator` in order; throws `IllegalArgumentException` if the size exceeds `Int.MaxValue`
issues.problem: Combines the return description with exception behavior. Scaladoc convention prefers a separate `@throws` tag for thrown exceptions, keeping the `@return` focused on the returned value.
issues.suggestion: a new array containing all elements of this `AnyAccumulator` in order (and add a separate `@throws IllegalArgumentException if the total size exceeds `Int.MaxValue`` tag)

issues.current_text: a collection of type `C1` containing all elements of this `AnyAccumulator`; throws `IllegalArgumentException` if the size exceeds `Int.MaxValue`
issues.problem: Same as above: mixes the return value with exception behavior. A dedicated `@throws` tag would be more idiomatic and discoverable in generated docs.
issues.suggestion: a collection of type `C1` containing all elements of this `AnyAccumulator` (and add a separate `@throws IllegalArgumentException if the total size exceeds `Int.MaxValue`` tag)

issues.current_text: the zero-based index of the element to retrieve, as a `Long`
issues.problem: The phrase "as a `Long`" is somewhat redundant because the parameter's declared type is already `Long`; the type annotation in the signature conveys this.
issues.suggestion: the zero-based index of the element to retrieve

general_feedback: The filled-in @return descriptions are accurate, concise, and consistent in tone with the surrounding documentation. @param and @tparam descriptions match actual usage and constraints. The main stylistic refinement would be to split out the `IllegalArgumentException` information from `@return` into dedicated `@throws` tags for `toArray` and `to`, which is the conventional Scaladoc pattern. No factual errors.
